raw_response: I agree with the consensus reached so far regarding Option A being correct. The WHO classification indeed uses separate codes like 506-509 for periapical lesions such as periapical cysts, whereas code 4.81 is designated for other forms of periodontal inflammation unrelated to the apex. Additionally, there isn't any support indicating that codes related directly to inflammatory changes would be represented differently than what was discussed—thus reinforcing the specificity and utility of each assigned code within the classification system. My agreement stands firm. Answer Choice: A.
